channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: The Blobby, Drippy Maximalist Design You’re Seeing Everywhere
item.description: After years of corporate millennial blanding, consumer culture oozes into shape for a new generation.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann

item.title: It’s Good Enough for the King. Is a Second Birthday Right for You?
item.description: A birthday is already an occasion for indulgence. So why not schedule one on your terms.
item.dc:creator: Louis Lucero II
item.media:description: Trooping the Color, an elaborate military parade to mark the monarch’s official birthday, takes place in June, though King Charles III, center, was born in November.

item.title: Highlights From the Las Culturistas Culture Awards Hosted by Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers
item.description: The comedians Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers honored the nooks and crannies of pop culture at the annual awards show.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Spencer

item.title: As Pitti Uomo’s Guest Designer, Simone Rocha Has Pretty Designs on Men
item.description: For over a decade, the Irish designer has found inspiration in womanhood. As the guest designer at Pitti Uomo, she plays with notions of masculinity.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.category: Central Saint Martins College of Art and Design
Dover Street Market New York (Manhattan, NY, Retailer)
item.media:credit: Jeremie Souteyrat for The New York Times

item.title: Taylor Swift Didn’t Marry in Rhode Island. Fans Showed Up Anyway.
item.description: Despite reports pointing to New York as a potential Swift-Kelce wedding location, Swifties still descended on Watch Hill, R.I., this past weekend, hoping for a glimpse of the singer or signs of a June 13 celebration.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Sarah Meftah for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pair of young Taylor Swift fans on East Beach, near the singer's home in Watch Hill. Eleven-year-old Nora Taraszkiewicz, right, said she’s been a fan “since birth.”

item.title: World Cup Fans Share Videos of Them Trying American Food. They’re a Hit.
item.description: Social media dispatches from travelers headed to matches appreciate the more mundane parts of American life. They’re a hit.
item.dc:creator: Melina Delkic

item.title: NY Liberty’s Ellie the Elephant Celebrates Her 6th Birthday in Style
item.description: Sure, there are bigger milestones than a sixth birthday. But the New York Liberty’s trunk-shaking mascot isn’t one to miss a chance to party.
item.dc:creator: Sandra E. Garcia and Justin J Wee
item.media:credit: Justin J Wee for The New York Times

item.title: A New Magazine With Political Heft, and ‘Real Style’
item.description: Equator, a new magazine with editors based across the globe, hosted a launch party in a ballroom in Manhattan’s Chinatown.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Schade
item.media:credit: Paola Chapdelaine for The New York Times
item.media:description: The cover of Equator’s inaugural issue.

item.title: The Look of Patriotism at Trump’s UFC Fight
item.description: From the athletes to the Octagon Girls, the Freedom 250 Flag Day event was a fashion show in red, white and blue.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Ilia Topuria, left, of Georgia and Justin Gaethje of the United States, decked out in red, white and blue for their UFC Lightweight Championship bout at the White House on Sunday.

item.title: I Have Fallen in Love With My Doctor
item.description: But we have a huge age gap.
item.dc:creator: W. Blake Martin

item.title: Neither Wanted Marriage. That Changed After Three Weeks Apart.
item.description: When Alison Greenberg, a rom-com writer, and Barry Loudis met, both were divorced with no desire to remarry. “Joke’s on us,” Greenberg said.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: It Was Smooth Sailing From the Start
item.description: When Matthew Casale, a lieutenant with the U.S. Navy, connected with Emily Sherron, he was instantly smitten.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Menza

item.title: On a Ride to Her Gate, She Found Her Husband
item.description: Dr. Aneesha Gala was waiting for an airport train when she ran into a classmate who was with her family. The classmate’s brother would become Gala’s husband.
item.dc:creator: Stella Raine Chu

item.title: The Wedding Was Part of a Show, and the Show Was Part of the Wedding
item.description: Pietro Alexander and Sara Maliki began a new chapter of their lives by getting married in the SoHo art gallery they opened moments later.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

item.title: He Gave Her a ‘Nontransferable’ Coupon for a Ring
item.description: Robert O’Connell, an interior designer, proposed to Aiyana Jaffe, a jewelry designer, with a 1957 silver box by Maria Pergay. He left the ring up to her.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: An Opera Performance With a Real-Life Happy Ending
item.description: Gideon Dabi and Alyssa Martin met five years ago while performing in a production of Rossini’s “La Cenerentola.” They quickly became friends.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Rama Duwaji Had the Winning Look at the Knicks Parade
item.description: The first lady and the mayor showed up for the celebration in style.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: Not your usual mayoral merch: Rama Duwaji is wearing a top by Claire Sullivan, made from upcycled Knicks T-shirts, at the Knicks championship parade.

item.title: Stephen Colbert Nods to Obama’s Tan Suit at Presidential Center Opening
item.description: Celebrity guests paid winking homage to President Obama’s notorious tan suit.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:description: A pair of funny guys in shades of khaki at the Obama presidential library opening on Thursday.

item.title: Danny Simmons, Painter and Activist From a Creative Family, Dies at 72
item.description: The older brother of the music mogul Russell Simmons and the rapper Joseph Simmons, he made his own way as an artistic and entrepreneurial force in Brooklyn.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.media:description: Danny Simmons at his home in Brooklyn in 2004. “Danny’s the fine artist” in the family, his brother Russell Simmons told The New York Times in 2004. “The real thing, a cultural hero.”

item.title: Yves Saint Laurent and Photography: A Hot and Heavy Romance
item.description: When fashion met image, both changed. A show at International Center of Photography shines a light on the relationship — but doesn’t really get the whole picture.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Ask the Therapist: A Family Estrangement Is Causing Even More Rifts
item.description: Our Ask the Therapist columnist, Lori Gottlieb, advises a reader whose family estrangement is causing more rifts.
item.dc:creator: Lori Gottlieb

item.title: What Young Men Thought of Trump’s U.F.C. Fight
item.description: The spectacle seemed targeted to the young male voters drifting away from the president. Some were skeptical.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Taylor Pemberton
item.media:description: Even those who liked the aesthetics of the U.F.C. Freedom 250 event on Sunday found it to be a distraction from more pressing issues.

item.title: The Good List: 6 Things to Bring Joy to Your Day
item.description: A memory workout, an endless playlist and an antidote to worry.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘Why Don’t You Both Get In?’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.

item.title: I Have Autism. Can I Tell My Best Friend That I Think She Does, Too?
item.description: A teenager who found comfort and clarity in an autism diagnosis wants to put a friend on a similar path, but is unsure how much encouragement is appropriate.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes

item.title: Mental Health Can Complicate Family Planning
item.description: Five stories show the hurdles that people with psychological diagnoses face when considering parenthood.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron
item.media:credit: Bethany Mollenkof for The New York Times
item.media:description: Raising young twins is “really challenging,” Ms. Bui said. “But we’re doing well, considering.”

item.title: Jelly Roll Files for Divorce From Bunnie Xo After Nearly a Decade
item.description: The country music star, whose real name is Jason DeFord, filed for divorce from his wife of nearly 10 years, Alisa DeFord, who is known as Bunnie Xo.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: Jelly Roll and Bunnie Xo at the Grammy Awards in February.

item.title: The World Cup (of Clothes)
item.description: Plus, a Knicks merch bonanza, a Japanese streetwear reunion and, surprise, it’s almost fashion week. Again.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: Tell Us Why Taylor Swift’s Wedding Is Meaningful to You
item.description: For an upcoming story, we want fans to talk to us about the journey they have been on with Swift through the years and how they feel about her big day.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens
item.media:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce will be tying the knot in the near future.

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Elevating Workwear With Whimsical Embroidery
item.description: A young woman in the Bay Area’s finance industry steps out in a different kind of work wear.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: DR Congo Soccer Team’s Leopard Suits Bring Pride to the World Cup
item.description: “In Congo culture, the spirit of leopard is a spirit of strength,” the designer, Alvin Mak, said of the ensembles that beguiled audiences online.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:description: The designer behind the team’s looks, seen on the player Fiston Mayele, cold-emailed the country’s ministry of sport.

item.title: He Made ‘Heathcliff’ Absurd
item.description: Peter Gallagher’s spin on a comic strip staple has found a passionate audience away from the funny pages.
item.dc:creator: Calum Marsh
item.media:credit: Sydney Krantz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peter Gallagher in his basement office, which he calls “the Dungeon.”

item.title: Flower Farms Are Emerging as Sought-After Wedding Venues
item.description: Flower farms are emerging as sought-after wedding venues, offering both a sustainable and photogenic backdrop.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Markoulakis

item.title: In Venezuela, a 91-Year-Old Weaver Sticks to Traditions
item.description: Though electric machines are now standard, the Venezuelan weaver Margarita Mora has clung to a mix of ancestral Indigenous and Spanish practices to create surprisingly modern work.
item.dc:creator: Silvia Benedetti and Andrea Hernández
item.media:credit: Andrea Hernandez for The New York Times

item.title: Soccer Fans Kick Off the World Cup in Style
item.description: At the U.S. match against Paraguay, the colors and the chants were loud in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: David Plowden, Who Photographed a Disappearing America, Dies at 93
item.description: With his haunting images of steam locomotives, steel mills and Midwestern farms, the celebrated lensman revealed the poetry in the artifacts of manual labor.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

item.title: It Was 1973, and the Knicks Were Heading to Game 5 …
item.description: Lifelong fans reminisced on the team’s last championship win. “When the Knicks won, it was like a different New York City,” one said.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Gershenson and Jesse McKinley
item.media:description: Dave DeBusschere in the 1973 N.B.A. Finals. The Knicks forward’s tenacious rebounding helped propel the franchise to its only two championships.

item.title: From New York to Texas, a Knicks Guard’s Braids Bring Style to the Court
item.description: Josh Hart is known for his intricate braids, which often feature New York’s logo. For the finals, he is working with hairstylists he has known for years.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:description: Hart sees the braids as a way to honor New York City.

item.title: Knicks Fever Is Shaping Weddings, Too
item.description: The overlap of the N.B.A. finals with peak wedding season is presenting a dilemma for some couples.
item.dc:creator: Alyson Krueger

item.title: Madeline Cash Isn’t Playing Around. Or Is She?
item.description: Her debut novel taps into a microgeneration’s blurring of performance and reality.
item.dc:creator: Mikkel Rosengaard
item.media:credit: Jono White for The New York Times
item.media:description: The 30-year-old novelist belongs to a downtown scene with a certain sensibility.
